posts.author: Abricoo (M.CH)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I really appreciate the Monday VIBE feature, which allows you to create table views. However, when the prompt fails, it becomes difficult to get it working correctly again, and it’s impossible to revert to a working version.
Do you have any tips or solutions to overcome this problem?

Capture d'écran 2025-12-15 093455370×324 21.8 KB

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Abricoo

Thank you for your feedback and for sharing your experience with the Monday VIBE feature. We are glad to hear that you find the table view creation useful. We understand how frustrating it can be when a prompt fails and it becomes difficult to recover or return to a previously working version.

At the moment a good practice is to save or duplicate a working prompt or app version before making significant changes so you always have a fallback. When a prompt fails, trying smaller incremental adjustments instead of a full rewrite can also help identify what caused the issue. If the tool allows version history or duplication of apps, using that feature regularly can reduce the risk of losing a stable configuration. Finally, reporting the failed prompt details to the support team can help them investigate and improve recovery and rollback options in future updates.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard

posts.author: amichay (Amichay Even Chen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! I’m Amichay from monday.com
We have a bug that prevents apps from being reverted to older versions, it should be fixed by tomorrow. Sorry of this experience, we are working hard to fix it

posts.author: Abricoo (M.CH)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, thank you for your feedback and for taking action
